# coding=utf-8
# Copyright 2024 The SpecForge team. All rights reserved.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
"""Training-object assembly driven by the application composition root.

The application resolves one immutable algorithm registration and passes it
through every builder.  This module never resolves an algorithm name and never
constructs an in-process online target engine; online capture comes exclusively
from an external SGLang server through the disaggregated runtime.

Heavy model/data dependencies stay lazy so importing :mod:`specforge.training`
does not load Transformers, datasets, or a target backend.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import json
import os
from collections import Counter
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Mapping, Optional

from specforge.algorithms.contracts import FeatureMode
from specforge.algorithms.registry import AlgorithmRegistration
from specforge.config import Config
from specforge.training.provenance import (
    model_resume_provenance as _model_resume_provenance,
)


@dataclass
class ModelBundle:
    """Objects and capture metadata needed by one configured training run."""

    model: Any
    draft_model: Any
    draft_config: Any
    input_tools: Any = None
    target_head: Any = None
    target_hidden_size: int = 0
    target_vocab_size: int = 0
    draft_vocab_size: int = 0
    capture_layers: Optional[List[int]] = None
    strategy_kwargs: Mapping[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)


@dataclass
class TrainingRun:
    """A fully assembled run with one lifecycle for rollout and training."""

    trainer: Any = None
    execute: Optional[Callable[[], int]] = None
    on_success: Optional[Callable[[int], None]] = None
    on_failure: Optional[Callable[[BaseException], None]] = None
    on_finally: Optional[Callable[[], None]] = None

    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
        if (self.trainer is None) == (self.execute is None):
            raise ValueError("a training run needs exactly one trainer or executor")
        if self.execute is not None and any(
            hook is not None
            for hook in (self.on_success, self.on_failure, self.on_finally)
        ):
            raise ValueError("lifecycle hooks belong to trainer-bearing runs only")

    def run(self) -> int:
        if self.execute is not None:
            return self.execute()
        try:
            result = self.trainer.fit()
            if self.on_success is not None:
                self.on_success(result)
            return result
        except BaseException as exc:
            if self.on_failure is not None:
                self.on_failure(exc)
            raise
        finally:
            if self.on_finally is not None:
                self.on_finally()


def _target_text_config(config):
    return getattr(config, "text_config", config)


def _torch_dtype(name: str):
    import torch

    return getattr(torch, name)


def _device():
    from specforge.utils import get_local_device

    return get_local_device()


def _load_draft(cfg: Config, algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration):
    """Construct the configured draft model without any legacy trainer code."""
    from specforge.modeling.draft.registry import resolve_draft
    from specforge.training.model_loading import resolve_draft_config

    provider = algorithm.providers.model
    draft_config = resolve_draft_config(cfg, provider=provider.draft_config)
    draft_model = provider.build_draft(cfg, draft_config)
    architecture = provider.draft_config.architecture
    expected_type = resolve_draft(architecture)
    if not isinstance(draft_model, expected_type):
        raise ValueError(
            f"training.strategy={algorithm.name!r} requires {architecture}, but "
            f"the resolved draft config builds "
            f"{type(draft_model).__name__}"
        )
    return draft_config, draft_model


def _load_text_tokenizer(cfg: Config):
    """Load tokenizer tooling used by current built-in text providers."""
    if cfg.model.input_modality != "text":
        raise ValueError(
            "built-in algorithms currently provide training-model input tooling "
            f"only for modality 'text', got {cfg.model.input_modality!r}; "
            "another modality must add its own input provider"
        )
    from transformers import AutoTokenizer

    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(
        cfg.model.target_model_path,
        cache_dir=cfg.model.cache_dir,
        trust_remote_code=cfg.model.trust_remote_code,
    )
    if cfg.model.tokenizer_pad_token_id is not None:
        tokenizer.pad_token_id = cfg.model.tokenizer_pad_token_id
    elif tokenizer.pad_token_id is None:
        fallback_id = tokenizer.eos_token_id
        if isinstance(fallback_id, (list, tuple)):
            fallback_id = fallback_id[0] if fallback_id else None

        if fallback_id is None:
            fallback_id = tokenizer.unk_token_id
        if fallback_id is None:
            raise ValueError(
                "target tokenizer has no pad, EOS, or unknown token ID; set "
                "model.tokenizer_pad_token_id explicitly"
            )
        tokenizer.pad_token_id = fallback_id
    return tokenizer


def _load_input_tools(
    cfg: Config,
    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
    *,
    input_adapter=None,
):
    """Load modality tooling through the provider port or the text default."""

    if input_adapter is None and cfg.mode == "online":
        streaming = algorithm.providers.server_streaming_for(cfg.model.input_modality)
        input_adapter = streaming.create_input_adapter(cfg)
    if input_adapter is not None:
        return input_adapter.load_input_tools(cfg)
    return _load_text_tokenizer(cfg)


def build_model_bundle(cfg: Config, *, algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration) -> ModelBundle:
    """Build the method-specific composite model and frozen target pieces."""
    import torch

    from specforge.modeling.target.target_utils import (
        load_target_config,
        target_text_config,
    )
    from specforge.modeling.target.target_utils import (
        target_vocab_size as resolve_target_vocab_size,
    )

    provider = algorithm.providers.model
    draft_config, draft_model = _load_draft(cfg, algorithm)
    needs_input_tools = provider.needs_input_tools(cfg, draft_model)
    input_tools = _load_input_tools(cfg, algorithm) if needs_input_tools else None
    target_config = load_target_config(
        cfg.model.target_model_path,
        cache_dir=cfg.model.cache_dir,
        trust_remote_code=cfg.model.trust_remote_code,
    )
    text_config = target_text_config(target_config)
    target_hidden_size = int(text_config.hidden_size)
    target_vocab_size = resolve_target_vocab_size(target_config)
    draft_vocab_size = int(
        getattr(draft_config, "draft_vocab_size", draft_config.vocab_size)
    )

    parts = provider.build_training_model(
        cfg, draft_model, draft_config, target_config, input_tools
    )
    if cfg.mode == "online" and parts.capture_layers is None:
        parts.capture_layers = provider.resolve_capture_layers(
            cfg, draft_config, target_config
        )

    # Keep the composite and target parts bf16 while avoiding accidental target
    # gradients. The optimizer still receives the strategy's trainable module.
    if parts.target_head is not None and isinstance(parts.target_head, torch.nn.Module):
        parts.target_head.requires_grad_(False)

    return ModelBundle(
        model=parts.model,
        draft_model=draft_model,
        draft_config=draft_config,
        input_tools=input_tools,
        target_head=parts.target_head,
        target_hidden_size=target_hidden_size,
        target_vocab_size=target_vocab_size,
        draft_vocab_size=draft_vocab_size,
        capture_layers=parts.capture_layers,
        # This mapping also carries the provider-bound checkpoint policy. It is
        # forwarded unchanged through every trainer-bearing topology.
        strategy_kwargs=algorithm.providers.step.bind_runtime(
            cfg,
            draft_model,
            parts.model,
            model_provenance=_model_resume_provenance(
                cfg,
                draft_config,
                target_config,
                capture_layers=parts.capture_layers,
            ),
        ),
    )


class _ConfiguredOptimizerFactory:
    def __init__(self, cfg: Config) -> None:
        self.cfg = cfg
        self.total_steps = cfg.training.total_steps or cfg.training.max_steps

    def configure_total_steps(self, total_steps: int) -> None:
        if self.total_steps is None:
            self.total_steps = total_steps
        elif self.total_steps != total_steps:
            raise ValueError(
                "optimizer/controller schedule mismatch: "
                f"{self.total_steps} != {total_steps}"
            )

    def __call__(self, draft_module):
        from specforge.optimizer import BF16Optimizer

        if self.total_steps is None:
            raise RuntimeError("optimizer total_steps was not resolved before assembly")
        t = self.cfg.training
        return BF16Optimizer(
            draft_module,
            lr=t.learning_rate,
            max_grad_norm=t.max_grad_norm,
            warmup_ratio=t.warmup_ratio,
            total_steps=self.total_steps,
            offload_master=t.optimizer_cpu_offload,
        )


def _optimizer_factory(cfg: Config):
    return _ConfiguredOptimizerFactory(cfg)


def _logger(metrics, step):
    printable = {}
    for key, value in metrics.items():
        try:
            printable[key] = float(value)
        except (TypeError, ValueError):
            try:
                printable[key] = [float(item) for item in value]
            except (TypeError, ValueError):
                continue
    print(f"step {step}: {printable}", flush=True)


def _configured_logger(cfg: Config):
    """Create an external tracker only for a trainer-bearing run."""
    if cfg.tracking.report_to == "none" or cfg.training.role == "producer":
        return _logger

    from types import SimpleNamespace

    from specforge.training.tracking import create_tracker_logger

    options = cfg.tracking.model_dump()
    options["wandb_project"] = options["wandb_project"] or "specforge"
    options["wandb_name"] = options["wandb_name"] or cfg.run_id
    options["swanlab_project"] = options["swanlab_project"] or "specforge"
    options["swanlab_name"] = options["swanlab_name"] or cfg.run_id
    options["mlflow_experiment_name"] = options["mlflow_experiment_name"] or "specforge"
    options["mlflow_run_name"] = options["mlflow_run_name"] or cfg.run_id
    return create_tracker_logger(
        SimpleNamespace(**options), cfg.output_dir, console_logger=_logger
    )


def _close_configured_logger(logger) -> None:
    close = getattr(logger, "close", None)
    if callable(close):
        close()


def _prompt_cache_key(cfg: Config, *, path: Optional[str] = None) -> str:
    source_path = path or cfg.data.prompts_path or cfg.data.train_data_path
    content_hash = None
    if source_path and os.path.isfile(source_path):
        source_hasher = hashlib.sha256()
        with open(source_path, "rb") as source_file:
            for chunk in iter(lambda: source_file.read(8 * 1024 * 1024), b""):
                source_hasher.update(chunk)
        content_hash = source_hasher.hexdigest()

    identity = {
        "path": source_path,
        "content_hash": content_hash,
        "max_length": cfg.data.max_length,
        "chat_template": cfg.data.chat_template,
        "is_preformatted": cfg.data.is_preformatted,
        "train_only_last_turn": cfg.data.train_only_last_turn,
        "max_prompts": cfg.data.max_prompts,
        "target_model": cfg.model.target_model_path,
        "draft_config": cfg.model.draft_model_config,
        "draft_checkpoint": cfg.model.draft_checkpoint_path,
        "draft_num_hidden_layers": cfg.model.draft_num_hidden_layers,
        "draft_block_size": cfg.model.draft_block_size,
        "strategy": cfg.training.strategy,
        "input_modality": cfg.model.input_modality,
    }
    return hashlib.sha256(json.dumps(identity, sort_keys=True).encode()).hexdigest()


def _prepare_prompts(
    cfg: Config,
    tokenizer,
    *,
    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
    draft_config,
    path: Optional[str] = None,
    cache_key: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[dict]:
    """Prepare one prompt source with an optional path/cache namespace override.

    Training keeps the configured cache key. Evaluation supplies its own path
    and derived key so it can never read or overwrite the training prompt cache.
    """
    if cfg.model.input_modality != "text":
        raise ValueError(
            "the built-in prompt preparer supports only modality 'text'; "
            f"algorithm {algorithm.name!r} must provide a ServerInputAdapter "
            f"for {cfg.model.input_modality!r}"
        )
    from specforge.data.prompt_builder import prepare_prompt_tasks

    configured_path = cfg.data.prompts_path or cfg.data.train_data_path
    source_path = path or configured_path
    if not source_path:
        raise ValueError("prompt preparation requires a non-empty data path")
    if cache_key is None:
        cache_key = (
            cfg.data.cache_key
            if path is None and cfg.data.cache_key is not None
            else _prompt_cache_key(cfg, path=source_path)
        )
    min_loss_tokens = algorithm.providers.model.minimum_loss_tokens(cfg, draft_config)
    return prepare_prompt_tasks(
        source_path,
        tokenizer,
        chat_template=cfg.data.chat_template,
        max_length=cfg.data.max_length,
        is_preformatted=cfg.data.is_preformatted,
        train_only_last_turn=cfg.data.train_only_last_turn,
        cache_dir=cfg.data.cache_dir,
        cache_key=cache_key,
        num_proc=cfg.data.build_dataset_num_proc,
        min_loss_tokens=min_loss_tokens,
        max_prompts=cfg.data.max_prompts,
    )


def _install_dataset_vocab_mapping(
    cfg: Config,
    bundle: ModelBundle,
    *,
    counts: Counter,
    dataset_identity: str,
) -> None:
    """Build, cache, and install one deterministic EAGLE vocabulary map."""
    if (
        cfg.model.vocab_mapping_path
        or bundle.draft_vocab_size == bundle.target_vocab_size
    ):
        return

    key = hashlib.sha256(
        (
            f"{dataset_identity}:{bundle.target_vocab_size}:"
            f"{bundle.draft_vocab_size}"
        ).encode()
    ).hexdigest()
    directory = os.path.join(cfg.data.cache_dir, "vocab_mapping")
    os.makedirs(directory, exist_ok=True)
    path = os.path.join(directory, f"{key}.pt")
    if os.path.exists(path):
        bundle.draft_model.load_vocab_mapping(path)
        return

    import torch

    from specforge.data.preprocessing import process_token_dict_to_mappings

    d2t, t2d = process_token_dict_to_mappings(
        counts,
        bundle.draft_vocab_size,
        bundle.target_vocab_size,
    )

    # Every rank derives the same mapping and installs it directly, so training
    # does not depend on a shared cache filesystem. Rank 0 alone persists the
    # reusable cache file, avoiding concurrent torch.save writes.
    bundle.draft_model.d2t.copy_(d2t)
    bundle.draft_model.t2d.copy_(t2d)
    bundle.draft_model.vocab_mapping_loaded = True
    distributed = (
        torch.distributed.is_available() and torch.distributed.is_initialized()
    )
    if not distributed or torch.distributed.get_rank() == 0:
        temporary = f"{path}.{os.getpid()}.tmp"
        torch.save({"d2t": d2t, "t2d": t2d}, temporary)
        os.replace(temporary, path)


def _ensure_offline_vocab_mapping(
    cfg: Config,
    bundle: ModelBundle,
    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
) -> None:
    """Derive a local-offline map from the exact feature ids and loss masks."""
    if FeatureMode.OFFLINE not in algorithm.providers.vocab_mapping_modes:
        return
    if (
        cfg.model.vocab_mapping_path
        or bundle.draft_vocab_size == bundle.target_vocab_size
    ):
        return

    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.offline_reader import list_feature_files
    from specforge.training.vocab_mapping import count_effective_feature_tokens

    identity_parts = []
    for path in list_feature_files(cfg.data.hidden_states_path):
        stat = os.stat(path)
        identity_parts.append((os.path.abspath(path), stat.st_size, stat.st_mtime_ns))
    identity = json.dumps(
        {
            "kind": "offline-features-v1",
            "files": identity_parts,
            "max_length": cfg.data.max_length,
        },
        sort_keys=True,
    )
    counts = count_effective_feature_tokens(
        cfg.data.hidden_states_path,
        max_length=cfg.data.max_length,
        target_vocab_size=bundle.target_vocab_size,
    )
    _install_dataset_vocab_mapping(
        cfg,
        bundle,
        counts=counts,
        dataset_identity=identity,
    )


def _dataloader_num_workers(cfg: Config, algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration) -> int:
    dataloader_num_workers = cfg.data.dataloader_num_workers
    if dataloader_num_workers is None:
        dataloader_num_workers = (
            algorithm.providers.model.default_dataloader_num_workers
        )
    return dataloader_num_workers


def _profiling_options(cfg: Config):
    from specforge.training.profiling import ProfilingOptions

    return ProfilingOptions(
        enabled=cfg.profiling.enabled,
        start_step=cfg.profiling.start_step,
        num_steps=cfg.profiling.num_steps,
        record_shapes=cfg.profiling.record_shapes,
    )


def _common_launch_kwargs(
    cfg: Config,
    bundle: ModelBundle,
    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
    *,
    logger=_logger,
) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    t = cfg.training
    # USP shards one logical sample over ``sp_size`` ranks.  Preserve the
    # legacy optimizer-window semantics: one user accumulation unit represents
    # a complete logical sequence, not one local sequence shard.
    accumulation_steps = t.accumulation_steps
    if t.attention_backend == "usp":
        accumulation_steps *= t.sp_ulysses_size * t.sp_ring_size
    return dict(
        algorithm=algorithm,
        modality=cfg.model.input_modality,
        optimizer_factory=_optimizer_factory(cfg),
        run_id=cfg.run_id,
        output_dir=cfg.output_dir,
        batch_size=t.batch_size,
        accumulation_steps=accumulation_steps,
        max_steps=t.max_steps,
        total_steps=t.total_steps,
        save_interval=t.save_interval,
        eval_interval=t.eval_interval,
        max_checkpoints=t.max_checkpoints,
        logger=logger,
        log_interval=t.log_interval,
        strategy_kwargs=bundle.strategy_kwargs,
        tp_size=t.tp_size,
        sp_ulysses_size=t.sp_ulysses_size,
        sp_ring_size=t.sp_ring_size,
        dataloader_num_workers=_dataloader_num_workers(cfg, algorithm),
        profiling_options=_profiling_options(cfg),
    )


def build_training_run(
    cfg: Config,
    *,
    algorithm: AlgorithmRegistration,
) -> TrainingRun:
    """Assemble one validated run from an already-resolved algorithm.

    Offline training may run in one process or with a disaggregated feature
    source.  Online training is always disaggregated and captures through
    external SGLang servers; colocated online execution is intentionally absent.
    """

    if algorithm.name != cfg.training.strategy:
        raise ValueError(
            "resolved algorithm does not match training.strategy: "
            f"{algorithm.name!r} != {cfg.training.strategy!r}"
        )

    t = cfg.training
    if t.role != "producer":
        import torch.distributed as dist

        cfg.validate_world_size(dist.get_world_size() if dist.is_initialized() else 1)

    if cfg.mode == "online" and cfg.deployment.mode != "disaggregated":
        raise ValueError(
            "online training is server-only and requires "
            "deployment.mode='disaggregated'"
        )

    if cfg.deployment.mode == "disaggregated":
        from specforge.training.disaggregated import build_disaggregated_run

        run_logger = _configured_logger(cfg)
        try:
            return build_disaggregated_run(
                cfg,
                algorithm=algorithm,
                build_model_bundle=lambda run_cfg: build_model_bundle(
                    run_cfg, algorithm=algorithm
                ),
                prepare_prompts=lambda run_cfg, tokenizer, **kwargs: _prepare_prompts(
                    run_cfg,
                    tokenizer,
                    algorithm=algorithm,
                    **kwargs,
                ),
                optimizer_factory=_optimizer_factory,
                logger=run_logger,
            )
        except BaseException:
            _close_configured_logger(run_logger)
            raise

    if cfg.mode != "offline":
        raise ValueError("colocated execution supports offline training only")

    bundle = build_model_bundle(cfg, algorithm=algorithm)
    from specforge.launch import build_offline_runtime

    _ensure_offline_vocab_mapping(cfg, bundle, algorithm)
    run_logger = _configured_logger(cfg)
    try:
        trainer = build_offline_runtime(
            hidden_states_path=cfg.data.hidden_states_path,
            eval_hidden_states_path=cfg.data.eval_hidden_states_path or None,
            draft_model=bundle.model,
            target_head=bundle.target_head,
            ttt_length=t.ttt_length,
            max_len=cfg.data.max_length,
            num_epochs=t.num_epochs,
            use_usp_preprocess=(t.attention_backend == "usp"),
            seed=t.seed,
            resume_from=t.resume_from,
            **_common_launch_kwargs(
                cfg,
                bundle,
                algorithm,
                logger=run_logger,
            ),
        )
    except BaseException:
        _close_configured_logger(run_logger)
        raise
    return TrainingRun(trainer=trainer)


__all__ = [
    "ModelBundle",
    "TrainingRun",
    "build_model_bundle",
    "build_training_run",
]

15	"""Draft configuration and weights-only initialization for unified training.
16	
17	This module deliberately keeps model initialization separate from training
18	resume.  A warm start can read draft weights from a Hugging Face checkpoint or
19	from SpecForge's shared ``training_state.pt`` payload, but it has no access to
20	an optimizer, scheduler, controller counters, or RNG state.
21	"""
22	
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import glob
26	import json
27	import logging
28	import os
29	from dataclasses import dataclass
30	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Dict, Literal, Optional, Tuple
31	
32	if TYPE_CHECKING:
33	    from transformers import PretrainedConfig
34	
35	    from specforge.algorithms.common.providers import DraftConfigProvider
36	    from specforge.config import Config
37	
38	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
39	
40	_CONFIG_FILE = "config.json"
41	_STATE_FILE = "training_state.pt"
42	
43	# These are the architecture fields the legacy EAGLE generator copied from the
44	# target, plus RoPE/attention fields needed by newer Qwen-family targets.  A
45	# whitelist avoids feeding a target-specific nested config into the registered
46	# Llama/Qwen3 draft classes.
47	_TARGET_ARCHITECTURE_FIELDS = (
48	    "vocab_size",
49	    "hidden_size",
50	    "num_attention_heads",
51	    "num_key_value_heads",
52	    "intermediate_size",
53	    "max_position_embeddings",
54	    "rms_norm_eps",
55	    "hidden_act",
56	    "initializer_range",
57	    "attention_bias",
58	    "attention_dropout",
59	    "head_dim",
60	    "rope_theta",
61	    "rope_scaling",
62	    "bos_token_id",
63	    "eos_token_id",
64	    "pad_token_id",
65	    "sliding_window",
66	    "use_sliding_window",
67	    "max_window_layers",
68	)
69	
70	
71	@dataclass(frozen=True)
72	class WarmStartReport:
73	    """Observable result of loading draft weights without training state."""
74	
75	    source: str
76	    checkpoint_format: Literal["specforge", "pretrained"]
77	    loaded_keys: int
78	    missing_keys: Tuple[str, ...]
79	    loaded_embedding: bool
80	
81	
82	def _without_file_uri(source: str) -> str:
83	    return source[len("file://") :] if source.startswith("file://") else source
84	
85	
86	def _looks_like_local_path(source: str) -> bool:
87	    return (
88	        source.startswith((".", "/", "~", "file://"))
89	        or source.endswith(".json")
90	        or os.path.exists(os.path.expanduser(_without_file_uri(source)))
91	    )
92	
93	
94	def _draft_config_from_dict(payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> "PretrainedConfig":
95	    # Importing the draft package registers every built-in architecture before
96	    # consulting the registry. Model modules keep accelerator-only dependencies
97	    # behind execution boundaries, so this remains safe in a CPU producer.
98	    import specforge.modeling.draft  # noqa: F401
99	    from specforge.modeling.draft.registry import DRAFT_REGISTRY, available_drafts
100	
101	    payload = dict(payload)
102	    architectures = payload.get("architectures") or []
103	    if not isinstance(architectures, (list, tuple)) or len(architectures) != 1:
104	        raise ValueError(
105	            "draft config must name exactly one architecture; " f"got {architectures!r}"
106	        )
107	    architecture = architectures[0]
108	    if architecture not in DRAFT_REGISTRY:
109	        raise ValueError(
110	            f"draft architecture {architecture!r} is not registered; "
111	            f"available: {available_drafts()}"
112	        )
113	
114	    payload["architectures"] = [architecture]
115	    payload["tie_word_embeddings"] = False
116	    payload.setdefault("use_cache", True)
117	    if payload.get("draft_vocab_size") is None:
118	        payload["draft_vocab_size"] = payload.get("vocab_size")
119	    return DRAFT_REGISTRY[architecture].config_class.from_dict(payload)
120	
121	
122	def load_draft_config_source(
123	    source: str,
124	    *,
125	    cache_dir: Optional[str] = None,
126	    trust_remote_code: bool = False,
127	) -> "PretrainedConfig":
128	    """Load a registered draft config from JSON, a directory, or an HF repo."""
129	
130	    if not source:
131	        raise ValueError("draft config source must be non-empty")
132	    local_source = os.path.abspath(os.path.expanduser(_without_file_uri(str(source))))
133	    if os.path.isfile(local_source):
134	        config_path = local_source
135	    elif os.path.isdir(local_source):
136	        config_path = os.path.join(local_source, _CONFIG_FILE)
137	        if not os.path.isfile(config_path):
138	            raise FileNotFoundError(
139	                f"draft model directory has no {_CONFIG_FILE}: {local_source}"
140	            )
141	    else:
142	        if _looks_like_local_path(str(source)):
143	            raise FileNotFoundError(f"draft model config does not exist: {source}")
144	        from transformers import AutoConfig
145	
146	        loaded = AutoConfig.from_pretrained(
147	            source,
148	            cache_dir=cache_dir,
149	            trust_remote_code=trust_remote_code,
150	        )
151	        return _draft_config_from_dict(loaded.to_dict())
152	
153	    try:
154	        with open(config_path, encoding="utf-8") as stream:
155	            payload = json.load(stream)
156	    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
157	        raise ValueError(f"invalid draft config JSON {config_path}: {exc}") from exc
158	    return _draft_config_from_dict(payload)
159	
160	
161	def _checkpoint_config_source(checkpoint_source: Optional[str]) -> Optional[str]:
162	    """Return a config-bearing warm-start source, if it can be identified."""
163	
164	    if not checkpoint_source:
165	        return None
166	    raw_source = str(checkpoint_source)
167	    local_source = os.path.abspath(os.path.expanduser(_without_file_uri(raw_source)))
168	    if os.path.isfile(local_source):
169	        if os.path.basename(local_source) == _CONFIG_FILE:
170	            return local_source
171	        sibling = os.path.join(os.path.dirname(local_source), _CONFIG_FILE)
172	        return sibling if os.path.isfile(sibling) else None
173	    if os.path.isdir(local_source):
174	        direct = os.path.join(local_source, _CONFIG_FILE)
175	        if os.path.isfile(direct):
176	            return direct
177	        # A run root may point at a future checkpoint layout that also persists
178	        # config.json.  Do not resolve or read training state just to find it.
179	        candidates = []
180	        for pointer in glob.glob(os.path.join(local_source, "*-latest")):
181	            candidate = os.path.join(os.path.realpath(pointer), _CONFIG_FILE)
182	            if os.path.isfile(candidate):
183	                candidates.append(candidate)
184	        if len(set(candidates)) == 1:
185	            return candidates[0]
186	        return None
187	    if _looks_like_local_path(raw_source):
188	        return None
189	    # A non-local warm-start source is an HF repository. Its config is the best
190	    # architecture contract unless the run supplies an explicit config source.
191	    return raw_source
192	
193	
194	def _target_text_config(config: "PretrainedConfig") -> "PretrainedConfig":
195	    return getattr(config, "text_config", config)
196	
197	
198	def _serializable_config_value(value: Any) -> Any:
199	    try:
200	        import torch
201	
202	        if isinstance(value, torch.dtype):
203	            return str(value).replace("torch.", "")
204	    except ImportError:
205	        pass
206	    return value
207	
208	
209	def _generate_draft_config(
210	    cfg: "Config", provider: "DraftConfigProvider"
211	) -> "PretrainedConfig":
212	    defaults = provider.target_defaults
213	    if defaults is None:
214	        raise ValueError(
215	            f"training.strategy={cfg.training.strategy!r} requires "
216	            "model.draft_model_config "
217	            "or a pretrained model.draft_checkpoint_path containing config.json"
218	        )
219	
220	    from specforge.modeling.target.target_utils import (
221	        load_target_config,
222	        target_text_config,
223	        target_vocab_size,
224	    )
225	
226	    target_config = load_target_config(
227	        cfg.model.target_model_path,
228	        cache_dir=cfg.model.cache_dir,
229	        trust_remote_code=cfg.model.trust_remote_code,
230	    )
231	    target_config = target_text_config(target_config)
232	    payload: Dict[str, Any] = {}
233	    for field in _TARGET_ARCHITECTURE_FIELDS:
234	        value = getattr(target_config, field, None)
235	        if value is not None:
236	            payload[field] = _serializable_config_value(value)
237	    payload["vocab_size"] = target_vocab_size(target_config)
238	
239	    required = [name for name in ("vocab_size", "hidden_size") if name not in payload]
240	    if required:
241	        raise ValueError(
242	            f"target config {cfg.model.target_model_path!r} cannot generate a "
243	            f"draft config; missing {required}"
244	        )
245	    payload["architectures"] = [provider.architecture]
246	    payload["model_type"] = defaults.model_type
247	    payload["num_hidden_layers"] = defaults.num_hidden_layers
248	    payload["tie_word_embeddings"] = False
249	    payload["use_cache"] = True
250	    if payload.get("pad_token_id") is None:
251	        payload["pad_token_id"] = 0
252	    if defaults.draft_vocab_size is not None:
253	        payload["draft_vocab_size"] = defaults.draft_vocab_size
254	    if defaults.populate is not None:
255	        defaults.populate(payload, target_config, cfg)
256	    return _draft_config_from_dict(payload)
257	
258	
259	def _apply_draft_overrides(
260	    cfg: "Config",
261	    draft_config: "PretrainedConfig",
262	    provider: "DraftConfigProvider",
263	) -> None:
264	    num_layers = cfg.model.draft_num_hidden_layers
265	    if num_layers is not None:
266	        draft_config.num_hidden_layers = num_layers
267	    if cfg.model.draft_block_size is not None:
268	        draft_config.block_size = cfg.model.draft_block_size
269	    if provider.apply_overrides is not None:
270	        provider.apply_overrides(cfg, draft_config)
271	
272	
273	def resolve_draft_config(
274	    cfg: "Config", *, provider: "DraftConfigProvider"
275	) -> "PretrainedConfig":
276	    """Resolve and validate the draft architecture for one typed run config."""
277	
278	    source = cfg.model.draft_model_config
279	    if not source:
280	        source = _checkpoint_config_source(cfg.model.draft_checkpoint_path)
281	    if source:
282	        draft_config = load_draft_config_source(
283	            source,
284	            cache_dir=cfg.model.cache_dir,
285	            trust_remote_code=cfg.model.trust_remote_code,
286	        )
287	    else:
288	        draft_config = _generate_draft_config(cfg, provider)
289	
290	    expected = provider.architecture
291	    architectures = list(getattr(draft_config, "architectures", None) or [])
292	    if architectures != [expected]:
293	        raise ValueError(
294	            f"training.strategy={cfg.training.strategy!r} requires draft "
295	            f"architecture {expected}, got {architectures!r}"
296	        )
297	    _apply_draft_overrides(cfg, draft_config, provider)
298	    return draft_config
299	
300	
301	def draft_config_dict(
302	    cfg: "Config", *, provider: "DraftConfigProvider"
303	) -> Dict[str, Any]:
304	    """JSON-compatible resolved draft config for capture-only producer roles."""
305	
306	    return resolve_draft_config(cfg, provider=provider).to_dict()
307	
308	
309	def _has_pretrained_weights(path: str) -> bool:
310	    names = (
311	        "model.safetensors",
312	        "model.safetensors.index.json",
313	        "pytorch_model.bin",
314	        "pytorch_model.bin.index.json",
315	    )
316	    return any(os.path.isfile(os.path.join(path, name)) for name in names)
317	
318	
319	def _runtime_state_file(source: str) -> Optional[str]:
320	    """Resolve SpecForge checkpoint storage without loading resume state."""
321	
322	    path = os.path.abspath(os.path.expanduser(_without_file_uri(str(source))))
323	    if os.path.isfile(path):
324	        return path if os.path.basename(path) == _STATE_FILE else None
325	    if not os.path.isdir(path):
326	        return None
327	
328	    direct = os.path.join(path, _STATE_FILE)
329	    if os.path.isfile(direct) and not _has_pretrained_weights(path):
330	        return direct
331	    has_run_pointers = bool(
332	        glob.glob(os.path.join(path, "*-latest"))
333	        or glob.glob(os.path.join(path, "*-step*"))
334	    )
335	    if not has_run_pointers:
336	        return None
337	
338	    from specforge.training.checkpoint import CheckpointManager
339	
340	    checkpoint_dir = CheckpointManager.resolve_resume_dir(path)
341	    return os.path.join(checkpoint_dir, _STATE_FILE)
342	
343	
344	def _load_specforge_draft_state(
345	    state_path: str, *, expected_strategy: str
346	) -> Dict[str, Any]:
347	    import torch
348	
349	    try:
350	        # The restricted unpickler accepts tensors and primitive metadata while
351	        # refusing arbitrary checkpoint objects. Only draft_state_dict is used.
352	        state = torch.load(state_path, map_location="cpu", weights_only=True)
353	    except Exception as exc:
354	        raise ValueError(
355	            f"cannot read weights-only SpecForge checkpoint {state_path}: {exc}"
356	        ) from exc
357	    if not isinstance(state, dict) or not isinstance(
358	        state.get("draft_state_dict"), dict
359	    ):
360	        raise ValueError(
361	            f"SpecForge warm-start checkpoint has no draft_state_dict: {state_path}"
362	        )
363	    saved_strategy = state.get("strategy")
364	    if saved_strategy is not None and saved_strategy != expected_strategy:
365	        raise ValueError(
366	            f"warm-start checkpoint {state_path} was written by strategy "
367	            f"{saved_strategy!r}; this run trains {expected_strategy!r}"
368	        )
369	    return state["draft_state_dict"]
370	
371	
372	def _load_pretrained_draft_state(
373	    source: str,
374	    *,
375	    draft_config: "PretrainedConfig",
376	    cache_dir: Optional[str],
377	    trust_remote_code: bool,
378	) -> Dict[str, Any]:
379	    from specforge.modeling.auto import AutoDraftModel
380	
381	    loaded, loading_info = AutoDraftModel.from_pretrained(
382	        source,
383	        config=draft_config,
384	        cache_dir=cache_dir,
385	        trust_remote_code=trust_remote_code,
386	        output_loading_info=True,
387	    )
388	    missing_from_source = set(loading_info.get("missing_keys") or [])
389	    state = {
390	        key: value.detach().cpu()
391	        for key, value in loaded.state_dict().items()
392	        if key not in missing_from_source
393	    }
394	    del loaded
395	    return state
396	
397	
398	def warm_start_draft_model(
399	    model: Any,
400	    source: str,
401	    *,
402	    draft_config: "PretrainedConfig",
403	    strategy: str,
404	    allow_missing_embedding: bool = False,
405	    cache_dir: Optional[str] = None,
406	    trust_remote_code: bool = False,
407	) -> WarmStartReport:
408	    """Load only draft weights, never optimizer/counters/RNG training state."""
409	
410	    runtime_state = _runtime_state_file(source)
411	    if runtime_state is not None:
412	        checkpoint_format: Literal["specforge", "pretrained"] = "specforge"
413	        state = _load_specforge_draft_state(runtime_state, expected_strategy=strategy)
414	    else:
415	        checkpoint_format = "pretrained"
416	        state = _load_pretrained_draft_state(
417	            source,
418	            draft_config=draft_config,
419	            cache_dir=cache_dir,
420	            trust_remote_code=trust_remote_code,
421	        )
422	
423	    if not state:
424	        raise ValueError(f"warm-start checkpoint {source!r} contains no draft weights")
425	    try:
426	        result = model.load_state_dict(state, strict=False)
427	    except RuntimeError as exc:
428	        raise ValueError(
429	            f"warm-start checkpoint {source!r} has incompatible draft tensor "
430	            f"shapes: {exc}"
431	        ) from exc
432	    loaded_keys = len(state) - len(result.unexpected_keys)
433	    if result.unexpected_keys or loaded_keys == 0:
434	        raise ValueError(
435	            f"warm-start checkpoint {source!r} does not match this draft model: "
436	            f"loaded={loaded_keys}/{len(state)}, "
437	            f"unexpected={sorted(result.unexpected_keys)}"
438	        )
439	    allowed_missing = set()
440	    if allow_missing_embedding:
441	        allowed_missing = {key for key in result.missing_keys if "embed" in key.lower()}
442	    required_missing = sorted(set(result.missing_keys) - allowed_missing)
443	    if required_missing:
444	        raise ValueError(
445	            f"warm-start checkpoint {source!r} is missing draft weights required "
446	            f"by this architecture: {required_missing}"
447	        )
448	
449	    report = WarmStartReport(
450	        source=str(source),
451	        checkpoint_format=checkpoint_format,
452	        loaded_keys=loaded_keys,
453	        missing_keys=tuple(sorted(result.missing_keys)),
454	        loaded_embedding=any("embed" in key.lower() for key in state),
455	    )
456	    logger.info(
457	        "Warm-started %d draft tensors from %s (%s); missing=%s",
458	        report.loaded_keys,
459	        report.source,
460	        report.checkpoint_format,
461	        list(report.missing_keys),
462	    )
463	    return report
464	
465	
466	__all__ = [
467	    "WarmStartReport",
468	    "draft_config_dict",
469	    "load_draft_config_source",
470	    "resolve_draft_config",
471	    "warm_start_draft_model",
472	]
473

9	"""Dataset vocabulary mappings shared by online and offline EAGLE training."""
10	
11	from __future__ import annotations
12	
13	from collections import Counter
14	from typing import Optional
15	
16	
17	def count_effective_feature_tokens(
18	    hidden_states_path: str,
19	    *,
20	    max_length: Optional[int] = None,
21	    target_vocab_size: Optional[int] = None,
22	) -> Counter:
23	    """Count loss-bearing tokens directly from prepared offline features.
24	
25	    Offline feature files already contain the exact ``input_ids`` and
26	    ``loss_mask`` used for training. Reading those tensors avoids requiring the
27	    original raw conversation dataset solely to rebuild a vocabulary map.
28	    """
29	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.feature_store import load_feature_file
30	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.offline_reader import list_feature_files
31	
32	    paths = list_feature_files(hidden_states_path)
33	    if not paths:
34	        raise ValueError(f"no offline feature files found under {hidden_states_path!r}")
35	
36	    counts: Counter = Counter()
37	    for path in paths:
38	        raw = load_feature_file(path)
39	        missing = [name for name in ("input_ids", "loss_mask") if name not in raw]
40	        if missing:
41	            raise KeyError(
42	                f"{path} cannot derive an EAGLE vocab mapping; missing {missing}"
43	            )
44	        input_ids = raw["input_ids"].reshape(-1)
45	        loss_mask = raw["loss_mask"].reshape(-1)
46	        if input_ids.numel() != loss_mask.numel():
47	            raise ValueError(
48	                f"{path} has {input_ids.numel()} input ids but "
49	                f"{loss_mask.numel()} loss-mask entries"
50	            )
51	        if max_length is not None:
52	            input_ids = input_ids[:max_length]
53	            loss_mask = loss_mask[:max_length]
54	        selected = input_ids[loss_mask.to(dtype=bool)]
55	        if selected.numel() == 0:
56	            continue
57	        token_ids, frequencies = selected.unique(return_counts=True)
58	        for token_id, frequency in zip(token_ids.tolist(), frequencies.tolist()):
59	            token_id = int(token_id)
60	            if token_id < 0:
61	                raise ValueError(f"{path} contains negative token id {token_id}")
62	            if target_vocab_size is not None and token_id >= target_vocab_size:
63	                raise ValueError(
64	                    f"{path} contains token id {token_id}, outside target vocab "
65	                    f"size {target_vocab_size}"
66	                )
67	            counts[token_id] += int(frequency)
68	    return counts
69	
70	
71	__all__ = ["count_effective_feature_tokens"]

# coding=utf-8
# Copyright 2024 The SpecForge team. All rights reserved.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
"""Dataset vocabulary mappings shared by online and offline EAGLE training."""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections import Counter
from typing import Optional


def count_effective_feature_tokens(
    hidden_states_path: str,
    *,
    max_length: Optional[int] = None,
    target_vocab_size: Optional[int] = None,
) -> Counter:
    """Count loss-bearing tokens directly from prepared offline features.

    Offline feature files already contain the exact ``input_ids`` and
    ``loss_mask`` used for training. Reading those tensors avoids requiring the
    original raw conversation dataset solely to rebuild a vocabulary map.
    """
    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.feature_store import load_feature_file
    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.offline_reader import list_feature_files

    paths = list_feature_files(hidden_states_path)
    if not paths:
        raise ValueError(f"no offline feature files found under {hidden_states_path!r}")

    counts: Counter = Counter()
    for path in paths:
        raw = load_feature_file(path)
        missing = [name for name in ("input_ids", "loss_mask") if name not in raw]
        if missing:
            raise KeyError(
                f"{path} cannot derive an EAGLE vocab mapping; missing {missing}"
            )
        input_ids = raw["input_ids"].reshape(-1)
        loss_mask = raw["loss_mask"].reshape(-1)
        if input_ids.numel() != loss_mask.numel():
            raise ValueError(
                f"{path} has {input_ids.numel()} input ids but "
                f"{loss_mask.numel()} loss-mask entries"
            )
        if max_length is not None:
            input_ids = input_ids[:max_length]
            loss_mask = loss_mask[:max_length]
        selected = input_ids[loss_mask.to(dtype=bool)]
        if selected.numel() == 0:
            continue
        token_ids, frequencies = selected.unique(return_counts=True)
        for token_id, frequency in zip(token_ids.tolist(), frequencies.tolist()):
            token_id = int(token_id)
            if token_id < 0:
                raise ValueError(f"{path} contains negative token id {token_id}")
            if target_vocab_size is not None and token_id >= target_vocab_size:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"{path} contains token id {token_id}, outside target vocab "
                    f"size {target_vocab_size}"
                )
            counts[token_id] += int(frequency)
    return counts


__all__ = ["count_effective_feature_tokens"]

1	# coding=utf-8
2	# Copyright 2024 The SpecForge team. All rights reserved.
3	#
4	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");

12	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	# limitations under the License.
15	"""Stable, scalable identities for frozen model inputs used on resume.
16	
17	Fresh runs record a versioned identity from weight-shard metadata and the
18	contents of small JSON descriptors.  They never stream every target shard just
19	to construct checkpoint metadata.  Rank 0 resolves that identity and broadcasts
20	it, so a distributed launch also avoids multiplying filesystem metadata work.
21	
22	Checkpoints written before the metadata format contain full SHA-256 identities.
23	Those identities remain resumable: validation reconstructs the legacy value on
24	rank 0 only and caches it by a size/timestamp/file-identity signature. A
25	successful resume then writes the current format into its next checkpoint.
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import hashlib
31	import json
32	import logging
33	import os
34	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping, Optional
35	
36	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
37	
38	MODEL_SOURCE_IDENTITY_FORMAT_FIELD = "source_identity_format"
39	MODEL_SOURCE_IDENTITY_FORMAT = "stat-v2"
40	
41	# stat-v1 additionally embedded st_ctime_ns/st_dev/st_ino, which are not stable
42	# across NFS/FUSE/overlay remounts or pod reschedules, so byte-identical weights
43	# on the same volume were rejected after a restart. stat-v1 checkpoints stay
44	# resumable by projecting their shard records onto the stat-v2 fields.
45	_LEGACY_STAT_V1_FORMAT = "stat-v1"
46	
47	_LEGACY_HASH_CACHE_VERSION = 1
48	_LEGACY_HASH_CACHE_DIRECTORY = ".specforge-provenance-cache"
49	_MODEL_SOURCE_FIELDS = ("target_model", "draft_config", "vocab_mapping")
50	_TRACKED_SUFFIXES = (
51	    ".bin",
52	    ".json",
53	    ".pt",
54	    ".pth",
55	    ".safetensors",
56	)
57	
58	
59	def _sha256_file(path: str) -> str:
60	    hasher = hashlib.sha256()
61	    with open(path, "rb") as stream:
62	        for chunk in iter(lambda: stream.read(16 * 1024 * 1024), b""):
63	            hasher.update(chunk)
64	    return hasher.hexdigest()
65	
66	
67	def _tracked_files(directory: str) -> list[tuple[str, str, os.stat_result]]:
68	    files = []
69	    for entry in os.scandir(directory):
70	        if not entry.is_file(follow_symlinks=True) or not entry.name.endswith(
71	            _TRACKED_SUFFIXES
72	        ):
73	            continue
74	        files.append((entry.name, entry.path, entry.stat(follow_symlinks=True)))
75	    return sorted(files, key=lambda item: item[0])
76	
77	
78	def _metadata_file_identity(
79	    path: str,
80	    name: str,
81	    stat: os.stat_result,
82	) -> tuple[Any, ...]:
83	    # Model/config index JSON is small and semantically important.  Hashing it
84	    # keeps exact descriptor provenance without reading multi-GB weight shards.
85	    if path.endswith(".json"):
86	        return ("sha256", name, stat.st_size, _sha256_file(path))
87	    # Binary shards use the rsync-style (name, size, mtime) quick check only.
88	    # ctime/device/inode are deliberately excluded: they change across
89	    # NFS/FUSE/overlay remounts and pod reschedules even when the bytes are
90	    # identical, so embedding them rejects exactly the resumes this contract
91	    # exists to serve. Content-level identity is still pinned by the sha256'd
92	    # JSON descriptors above (config + safetensors index cover shard layout).
93	    return (
94	        "stat",
95	        name,
96	        stat.st_size,
97	        stat.st_mtime_ns,
98	    )
99	
100	
101	def model_source_identity(path: Optional[str]) -> Any:
102	    """Return the current cheap identity for one local or referenced source."""
103	
104	    if not path:
105	        return None
106	    expanded = os.path.abspath(os.path.expanduser(path))
107	    if not os.path.exists(expanded):
108	        return ("reference", path)
109	    if os.path.isfile(expanded):
110	        return (
111	            "file-stat-v2",
112	            expanded,
113	            _metadata_file_identity(expanded, "", os.stat(expanded)),
114	        )
115	
116	    files = tuple(
117	        _metadata_file_identity(file_path, name, stat)
118	        for name, file_path, stat in _tracked_files(expanded)
119	    )
120	    return ("directory-stat-v2", expanded, files)
121	
122	
123	def _legacy_model_source_identity_uncached(path: Optional[str]) -> Any:
124	    """Reproduce the pre-stat-v1 identity byte-for-byte."""
125	
126	    def file_identity(file_path: str, name: str) -> tuple[str, int, str]:
127	        return (name, os.path.getsize(file_path), _sha256_file(file_path))
128	
129	    if not path:
130	        return None
131	    expanded = os.path.abspath(os.path.expanduser(path))
132	    if not os.path.exists(expanded):
133	        return ("reference", path)
134	    if os.path.isfile(expanded):
135	        return ("file", expanded, file_identity(expanded, ""))
136	
137	    files = [
138	        file_identity(file_path, name)
139	        for name, file_path, _stat in _tracked_files(expanded)
140	    ]
141	    return ("directory", expanded, tuple(sorted(files)))
142	
143	
144	def _source_stat_signature(path: Optional[str]) -> Any:
145	    if not path:
146	        return None
147	    expanded = os.path.abspath(os.path.expanduser(path))
148	    if not os.path.exists(expanded):
149	        return ("reference", path)
150	
151	    def record(name: str, stat: os.stat_result) -> tuple[Any, ...]:
152	        return (
153	            name,
154	            stat.st_size,
155	            stat.st_mtime_ns,
156	            stat.st_ctime_ns,
157	            stat.st_dev,
158	            stat.st_ino,
159	        )
160	
161	    if os.path.isfile(expanded):
162	        return ("file", expanded, record("", os.stat(expanded)))
163	    return (
164	        "directory",
165	        expanded,
166	        tuple(record(name, stat) for name, _path, stat in _tracked_files(expanded)),
167	    )
168	
169	
170	def _freeze_json(value: Any) -> Any:
171	    if isinstance(value, list):
172	        return tuple(_freeze_json(item) for item in value)
173	    if isinstance(value, dict):
174	        return {key: _freeze_json(item) for key, item in value.items()}
175	    return value
176	
177	
178	def _legacy_cache_path(cache_root: str, signature: Any) -> str:
179	    encoded = json.dumps(signature, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":")).encode()
180	    key = hashlib.sha256(encoded).hexdigest()
181	    return os.path.join(cache_root, _LEGACY_HASH_CACHE_DIRECTORY, f"{key}.json")
182	
183	
184	def _read_legacy_cache(path: str, signature: Any) -> Any:
185	    try:
186	        with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as stream:
187	            payload = json.load(stream)
188	    except (FileNotFoundError, OSError, ValueError):
189	        return None
190	    if (
191	        payload.get("version") != _LEGACY_HASH_CACHE_VERSION
192	        or _freeze_json(payload.get("signature")) != signature
193	        or "identity" not in payload
194	    ):
195	        return None
196	    return _freeze_json(payload["identity"])
197	
198	
199	def _write_legacy_cache(path: str, signature: Any, identity: Any) -> None:
200	    temporary = f"{path}.{os.getpid()}.tmp"
201	    try:
202	        os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(path), exist_ok=True)
203	        with open(temporary, "w", encoding="utf-8") as stream:
204	            json.dump(
205	                {
206	                    "version": _LEGACY_HASH_CACHE_VERSION,
207	                    "signature": signature,
208	                    "identity": identity,
209	                },
210	                stream,
211	                sort_keys=True,
212	            )
213	        os.replace(temporary, path)
214	    except OSError as exc:
215	        logger.debug("could not persist model-provenance cache %s: %s", path, exc)
216	        try:
217	            os.remove(temporary)
218	        except OSError:
219	            pass
220	
221	
222	def legacy_model_source_identity(path: Optional[str], *, cache_root: str) -> Any:
223	    """Return the old full-content identity, reusing a safe stat-keyed cache."""
224	
225	    signature = _source_stat_signature(path)
226	    cache_path = _legacy_cache_path(cache_root, signature)
227	    cached = _read_legacy_cache(cache_path, signature)
228	    if cached is not None:
229	        return cached
230	    identity = _legacy_model_source_identity_uncached(path)
231	    _write_legacy_cache(cache_path, signature, identity)
232	    return identity
233	
234	
235	def _rank_zero_value(factory: Callable[[], Any], *, operation: str) -> Any:
236	    """Run filesystem work on rank 0 and broadcast either its value or error."""
237	
238	    import torch.distributed as dist
239	
240	    distributed = (
241	        dist.is_available() and dist.is_initialized() and dist.get_world_size() > 1
242	    )
243	    if not distributed:
244	        return factory()
245	
246	    payload = [None]
247	    if dist.get_rank() == 0:
248	        try:
249	            payload[0] = ("ok", factory())
250	        except Exception as exc:
251	            payload[0] = ("error", type(exc).__name__, str(exc))
252	    dist.broadcast_object_list(payload, src=0)
253	    result = payload[0]
254	    if not isinstance(result, tuple) or not result:
255	        raise RuntimeError(f"rank-0 {operation} returned a malformed broadcast")
256	    if result[0] == "error":
257	        raise RuntimeError(f"rank-0 {operation} failed with {result[1]}: {result[2]}")
258	    if result[0] != "ok" or len(result) != 2:
259	        raise RuntimeError(f"rank-0 {operation} returned a malformed broadcast")
260	    return result[1]
261	
262	
263	def _compute_model_resume_provenance(
264	    cfg: Any,
265	    draft_config: Any,
266	    target_config: Any,
267	    *,
268	    capture_layers: Optional[list[int]],
269	) -> dict[str, Any]:
270	    draft_source = cfg.model.draft_model_config or getattr(
271	        draft_config,
272	        "_name_or_path",
273	        None,
274	    )
275	    return {
276	        MODEL_SOURCE_IDENTITY_FORMAT_FIELD: MODEL_SOURCE_IDENTITY_FORMAT,
277	        "target_model": model_source_identity(cfg.model.target_model_path),
278	        "target_revision": getattr(target_config, "_commit_hash", None),
279	        "draft_config": model_source_identity(draft_source),
280	        "draft_revision": getattr(draft_config, "_commit_hash", None),
281	        "vocab_mapping": model_source_identity(cfg.model.vocab_mapping_path or None),
282	        "embedding_key": cfg.model.embedding_key,
283	        "lm_head_key": cfg.model.lm_head_key,
284	        "load_target_embedding": cfg.model.load_target_embedding,
285	        "capture_layers": (
286	            tuple(int(layer_id) for layer_id in capture_layers)
287	            if capture_layers is not None
288	            else None
289	        ),
290	        "input_modality": cfg.model.input_modality,
291	        "torch_dtype": cfg.model.torch_dtype,
292	    }
293	
294	
295	def model_resume_provenance(
296	    cfg: Any,
297	    draft_config: Any,
298	    target_config: Any,
299	    *,
300	    capture_layers: Optional[list[int]],
301	) -> dict[str, Any]:
302	    """Resolve one rank-agreed current provenance mapping."""
303	
304	    return _rank_zero_value(
305	        lambda: _compute_model_resume_provenance(
306	            cfg,
307	            draft_config,
308	            target_config,
309	            capture_layers=capture_layers,
310	        ),
311	        operation="model provenance resolution",
312	    )
313	
314	
315	def _mapping(value: Any, *, name: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
316	    if not isinstance(value, (Mapping, tuple, list)):
317	        raise ValueError(
318	            f"{name} model provenance is malformed: {type(value).__name__}"
319	        )
320	    try:
321	        return dict(value)
322	    except (TypeError, ValueError) as exc:
323	        raise ValueError(f"{name} model provenance is malformed") from exc
324	
325	
326	def _legacy_source_from_current(identity: Any, *, cache_root: str) -> Any:
327	    if identity is None:
328	        return None
329	    if not isinstance(identity, (tuple, list)) or not identity:
330	        raise ValueError(f"current model source identity is malformed: {identity!r}")
331	    kind = identity[0]
332	    if kind == "reference":
333	        return tuple(identity)
334	    if kind not in {"file-stat-v2", "directory-stat-v2"} or len(identity) < 2:
335	        raise ValueError(f"unsupported current model source identity: {identity!r}")
336	    return legacy_model_source_identity(identity[1], cache_root=cache_root)
337	
338	
339	def _stat_v1_source_to_v2(identity: Any) -> Any:
340	    """Project one stat-v1 source identity onto its remount-stable fields."""
341	
342	    if identity is None:
343	        return None
344	    if not isinstance(identity, (tuple, list)) or not identity:
345	        raise ValueError(f"saved model source identity is malformed: {identity!r}")
346	    kind = identity[0]
347	    if kind == "reference":
348	        return tuple(identity)
349	
350	    def project(record: Any) -> tuple[Any, ...]:
351	        record = tuple(record)
352	        if record and record[0] == "sha256":
353	            return record
354	        if record and record[0] == "stat" and len(record) >= 4:
355	            # ("stat", name, size, mtime_ns, ctime_ns, dev, ino) -> v2 prefix.
356	            return record[:4]
357	        raise ValueError(f"unsupported stat-v1 file identity: {record!r}")
358	
359	    if kind == "file-stat-v1" and len(identity) == 3:
360	        return ("file-stat-v2", identity[1], project(identity[2]))
361	    if kind == "directory-stat-v1" and len(identity) == 3:
362	        return (
363	            "directory-stat-v2",
364	            identity[1],
365	            tuple(project(record) for record in identity[2]),
366	        )
367	    raise ValueError(f"unsupported stat-v1 model source identity: {identity!r}")
368	
369	
370	def model_provenance_for_resume_comparison(
371	    current: Any,
372	    saved: Any,
373	    *,
374	    cache_root: str,
375	) -> tuple[Any, Any]:
376	    """Return ``(current, saved)`` adapted onto one comparable format.
377	
378	    Current checkpoints compare their metadata identities directly.  A saved
379	    stat-v1 mapping is projected onto the remount-stable stat-v2 fields.  A
380	    saved mapping with no format field is the legacy full-content contract;
381	    reconstruct that exact value lazily so existing checkpoints remain
382	    resumable.
383	    """
384	
385	    current_mapping = _mapping(current, name="current")
386	    saved_mapping = _mapping(saved, name="saved")
387	    current_format = current_mapping.get(MODEL_SOURCE_IDENTITY_FORMAT_FIELD)
388	    saved_format = saved_mapping.get(MODEL_SOURCE_IDENTITY_FORMAT_FIELD)
389	
390	    if current_format is None:
391	        return current, saved
392	    if current_format != MODEL_SOURCE_IDENTITY_FORMAT:
393	        raise ValueError(
394	            f"unsupported current model provenance format {current_format!r}"
395	        )
396	    if saved_format == current_format:
397	        return current, saved
398	    if saved_format == _LEGACY_STAT_V1_FORMAT:
399	        projected = dict(saved_mapping)
400	        projected[MODEL_SOURCE_IDENTITY_FORMAT_FIELD] = MODEL_SOURCE_IDENTITY_FORMAT
401	        for field in _MODEL_SOURCE_FIELDS:
402	            projected[field] = _stat_v1_source_to_v2(projected.get(field))
403	        return current, projected
404	    if saved_format is not None:
405	        raise ValueError(
406	            "checkpoint model provenance uses unsupported identity format "
407	            f"{saved_format!r}; expected {MODEL_SOURCE_IDENTITY_FORMAT!r}"
408	        )
409	
410	    def legacy_value() -> tuple[tuple[str, Any], ...]:
411	        migrated = dict(current_mapping)
412	        migrated.pop(MODEL_SOURCE_IDENTITY_FORMAT_FIELD)
413	        for field in _MODEL_SOURCE_FIELDS:
414	            migrated[field] = _legacy_source_from_current(
415	                migrated.get(field),
416	                cache_root=cache_root,
417	            )
418	        return tuple((key, migrated[key]) for key in sorted(migrated))
419	
420	    return (
421	        _rank_zero_value(
422	            legacy_value,
423	            operation="legacy model provenance hashing",
424	        ),
425	        saved,
426	    )
427	
428	
429	__all__ = [
430	    "MODEL_SOURCE_IDENTITY_FORMAT",
431	    "MODEL_SOURCE_IDENTITY_FORMAT_FIELD",
432	    "legacy_model_source_identity",
433	    "model_provenance_for_resume_comparison",
434	    "model_resume_provenance",
435	    "model_source_identity",
436	]
437

1	# Training Plane Design (`specforge.training`)
2	
3	This is the design note for the **training**, scoped to this plane.
4	The cross-plane picture (whole-system map, endpoint reference, autonomy) lives in
5	[`../runtime/ARCHITECTURE.md`](../runtime/ARCHITECTURE.md); the shared records
6	every plane exchanges are in
7	[`../runtime/contracts.py`](../runtime/contracts.py).
8	
9	## Responsibility
10	
11	Owns the one caller-facing `Trainer` lifecycle that turns a normalized,
12	tensor-carrying `TrainBatch` stream into optimizer steps and checkpoints.
13	`Trainer.fit()` is the only package-level training call: it enters any
14	topology-owned stream context, invokes the internal `TrainerController` over its
15	private loader, exits the context, and writes the final checkpoint when the
16	last optimizer step was not already saved by the periodic checkpoint path.
17	For the disaggregated online consumer, that same lifecycle then publishes its
18	terminal done/failed signal and always stops the rank-0 ref distributor. Direct
19	Python builder callers therefore receive the same cleanup guarantees as the
20	CLI; there is no second wrapper-owned training lifecycle.
21	
22	Below that boundary, `TrainerController` owns the epoch loop, optimizer-step
23	counting, interval checkpoints, and durable acknowledgements;
24	`TrainerCore` owns one branch-free train step and the accumulation boundary;
25	`DraftTrainStrategy` owns model-specific validation, forward/loss, target
26	projection, and checkpoint filtering; `FSDPTrainingBackend` owns wrapping,
27	backward, optimizer steps, distributed gradient norms, and full training state.
28	Checkpoint rotation and the latest pointer live in
29	`specforge.training.checkpoint`. Resume restores each rank's optimizer/RNG
30	state and repositions fixed offline refs through `FeatureDataLoader.seek()`.
31	
32	## Internal mechanics
33	
34	```mermaid
35	flowchart TD
36	  classDef compute fill:#e6f6ea,stroke:#3bb061,color:#0b4a22;
37	  classDef control fill:#e8f0fe,stroke:#3b6fd6,color:#0b2e6b;
38	
39	  PUB[Trainer fit public lifecycle] -->|private loader| FIT[TrainerController epoch loop]
40	  PUB -->|final unsaved step| CKPT[save_checkpoint collective]
41	  FIT -->|for batch in loader| TS[TrainerCore train_step]
42	  FIT -->|append sample_ids| PA[pending_ack accumulator]
43	  TS -->|forward_loss| ST[DraftTrainStrategy projection plus loss]
44	  TS -->|backward| BK[FSDPTrainingBackend]
45	  TS -->|step at accum boundary| BK
46	  TS -->|StepResult optimizer_stepped| FIT
47	  FIT -->|on boundary global_step plus 1| BND[boundary actions]
48	  BND -->|ack_fn ids step| ACK[ack_train_refs durable]
49	  BND -->|save_interval| CKPT
50	  CKPT -->|checkpoint_state_filter| ST
51	  CKPT -->|state_dict FULL| BK
52	
53	  class PUB,FIT,TS,ST,BK,BND,PA,CKPT compute;
54	  class ACK control;
55	```
56	
57	The training plane is the only tensor-carrying side besides the data plane; it
58	consumes `TrainBatch.tensors`. `TrainerCore.train_step` divides the loss by
59	`accumulation_steps`, uses `no_sync()` only for non-boundary micro-batches, and
60	returns `optimizer_stepped` as the single authoritative boundary signal.
61	`TrainerController` increments `global_step` only at that boundary, commits the
62	pending sample acknowledgements, emits metrics, and performs configured
63	interval saves. The outer `Trainer` owns topology cleanup and the guarded final
64	save, so CLI, builders, and Python callers cannot select a second loader-based
65	training entry. All saves delegate to `CheckpointManager`; the shared draft
66	state is written by rank 0 while every rank writes its own optimizer/RNG state.
67	
68	Natural end-of-stream is accepted only at an optimizer boundary. If the final
69	backward is inside FSDP `no_sync`, `fit` fails instead of stepping unreduced
70	gradients or reporting a checkpoint as successful. Queue-mode loaders
71	terminally settle and clean a short `drop_last` batch without emitting it;
72	fixed offline refs keep normal `drop_last` semantics.
73	
74	## Endpoints
75	
76	### What this plane calls into
77	
78	| From | Endpoint | Plane |
79	|---|---|---|
80	| `TrainerController` | `FeatureDataLoader.__iter__` | compute |
81	| `TrainerController` | `TrainerCore.train_step` | compute |
82	| `TrainerController` | `DataFlowController.ack_train_refs` | control |
83	| `TrainerCore` | `Eagle3TrainStrategy.forward_loss` | compute |
84	| `TrainerCore` | `FSDPTrainingBackend.backward` | compute |
85	| `TrainerCore` | `FSDPTrainingBackend.step` | compute |
86

# Training Plane Design (`specforge.training`)

This is the design note for the **training**, scoped to this plane.
The cross-plane picture (whole-system map, endpoint reference, autonomy) lives in
[`../runtime/ARCHITECTURE.md`](../runtime/ARCHITECTURE.md); the shared records
every plane exchanges are in
[`../runtime/contracts.py`](../runtime/contracts.py).

## Responsibility

Owns the one caller-facing `Trainer` lifecycle that turns a normalized,
tensor-carrying `TrainBatch` stream into optimizer steps and checkpoints.
`Trainer.fit()` is the only package-level training call: it enters any
topology-owned stream context, invokes the internal `TrainerController` over its
private loader, exits the context, and writes the final checkpoint when the
last optimizer step was not already saved by the periodic checkpoint path.
For the disaggregated online consumer, that same lifecycle then publishes its
terminal done/failed signal and always stops the rank-0 ref distributor. Direct
Python builder callers therefore receive the same cleanup guarantees as the
CLI; there is no second wrapper-owned training lifecycle.

Below that boundary, `TrainerController` owns the epoch loop, optimizer-step
counting, interval checkpoints, and durable acknowledgements;
`TrainerCore` owns one branch-free train step and the accumulation boundary;
`DraftTrainStrategy` owns model-specific validation, forward/loss, target
projection, and checkpoint filtering; `FSDPTrainingBackend` owns wrapping,
backward, optimizer steps, distributed gradient norms, and full training state.
Checkpoint rotation and the latest pointer live in
`specforge.training.checkpoint`. Resume restores each rank's optimizer/RNG
state and repositions fixed offline refs through `FeatureDataLoader.seek()`.

## Internal mechanics

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  classDef compute fill:#e6f6ea,stroke:#3bb061,color:#0b4a22;
  classDef control fill:#e8f0fe,stroke:#3b6fd6,color:#0b2e6b;

  PUB[Trainer fit public lifecycle] -->|private loader| FIT[TrainerController epoch loop]
  PUB -->|final unsaved step| CKPT[save_checkpoint collective]
  FIT -->|for batch in loader| TS[TrainerCore train_step]
  FIT -->|append sample_ids| PA[pending_ack accumulator]
  TS -->|forward_loss| ST[DraftTrainStrategy projection plus loss]
  TS -->|backward| BK[FSDPTrainingBackend]
  TS -->|step at accum boundary| BK
  TS -->|StepResult optimizer_stepped| FIT
  FIT -->|on boundary global_step plus 1| BND[boundary actions]
  BND -->|ack_fn ids step| ACK[ack_train_refs durable]
  BND -->|save_interval| CKPT
  CKPT -->|checkpoint_state_filter| ST
  CKPT -->|state_dict FULL| BK

  class PUB,FIT,TS,ST,BK,BND,PA,CKPT compute;
  class ACK control;
```

The training plane is the only tensor-carrying side besides the data plane; it
consumes `TrainBatch.tensors`. `TrainerCore.train_step` divides the loss by
`accumulation_steps`, uses `no_sync()` only for non-boundary micro-batches, and
returns `optimizer_stepped` as the single authoritative boundary signal.
`TrainerController` increments `global_step` only at that boundary, commits the
pending sample acknowledgements, emits metrics, and performs configured
interval saves. The outer `Trainer` owns topology cleanup and the guarded final
save, so CLI, builders, and Python callers cannot select a second loader-based
training entry. All saves delegate to `CheckpointManager`; the shared draft
state is written by rank 0 while every rank writes its own optimizer/RNG state.

Natural end-of-stream is accepted only at an optimizer boundary. If the final
backward is inside FSDP `no_sync`, `fit` fails instead of stepping unreduced
gradients or reporting a checkpoint as successful. Queue-mode loaders
terminally settle and clean a short `drop_last` batch without emitting it;
fixed offline refs keep normal `drop_last` semantics.

## Endpoints

### What this plane calls into

| From | Endpoint | Plane |
|---|---|---|
| `TrainerController` | `FeatureDataLoader.__iter__` | compute |
| `TrainerController` | `TrainerCore.train_step` | compute |
| `TrainerController` | `DataFlowController.ack_train_refs` | control |
| `TrainerCore` | `Eagle3TrainStrategy.forward_loss` | compute |
| `TrainerCore` | `FSDPTrainingBackend.backward` | compute |
| `TrainerCore` | `FSDPTrainingBackend.step` | compute |

1	# coding=utf-8
2	# Copyright 2024 The SpecForge team. All rights reserved.
3	#
4	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");

7	#
8	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	"""Disaggregated run assembly used by ``specforge train``.
10	
11	The role is configuration, not a different Python entry point. Producer and
12	consumer processes load the same run file with ``training.role`` set to their
13	respective role. Cross-process transport values intentionally remain environment
14	variables because they are deployment secrets/addresses rather than model
15	hyperparameters.
16	"""
17	
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	import json
21	import os
22	import time
23	from typing import Callable, Optional, Sequence
24	
25	from specforge.algorithms.registry import AlgorithmRegistration
26	from specforge.config import Config
27	
28	_PRODUCER_CLAIM_SUFFIX = ".producer_claim"
29	_ONLINE_SCHEDULE_SUFFIX = ".schedule.json"
30	_ONLINE_CONTROL_SUFFIXES = (
31	    ".closed",
32	    ".consumed_count",
33	    ".failed",
34	    ".consumer_done",
35	    ".consumer_failed",
36	    ".consumer_quantum",
37	    _ONLINE_SCHEDULE_SUFFIX,
38	)
39	_OFFLINE_CONTROL_SUFFIXES = (".done", ".consumed", ".failed", ".consumer_failed")
40	
41	
42	def _write_control(path: str, value: str = "") -> None:
43	    """Atomically publish one small filesystem control record."""
44	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(path)), exist_ok=True)
45	    tmp = f"{path}.tmp.{os.getpid()}"
46	    with open(tmp, "w", encoding="utf-8") as stream:
47	        stream.write(value)
48	        stream.flush()
49	        os.fsync(stream.fileno())
50	    os.replace(tmp, path)
51	
52	
53	def _read_control(path: str) -> Optional[str]:
54	    try:
55	        with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as stream:
56	            return stream.read().strip() or "unknown remote failure"
57	    except FileNotFoundError:
58	        return None
59	
60	
61	def _publish_control_failure(path: str, exc: BaseException) -> None:
62	    """Best-effort peer notification that never hides the root exception."""
63	    try:
64	        _write_control(path, f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
65	    except Exception as signal_exc:
66	        print(
67	            f"failed to publish role failure to {path}: {signal_exc}",
68	            flush=True,
69	        )
70	
71	
72	def _claim_fresh_control_path(path: str, suffixes: Sequence[str]) -> None:
73	    """Reject artifact reuse and atomically claim a new producer attempt."""
74	    claim = path + _PRODUCER_CLAIM_SUFFIX
75	    artifacts = [path, claim, *(path + suffix for suffix in suffixes)]
76	    existing = [item for item in artifacts if os.path.exists(item)]
77	    if existing:
78	        raise ValueError(
79	            f"control path {path!r} has artifacts from an existing attempt: "
80	            f"{existing}; choose a new attempt-specific path"
81	        )
82	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(path)), exist_ok=True)
83	    try:
84	        fd = os.open(claim, os.O_CREAT | os.O_EXCL | os.O_WRONLY, 0o644)
85	    except FileExistsError as exc:
86	        raise ValueError(
87	            f"control path {path!r} is already claimed by another producer; "
88	            "choose a new attempt-specific path"
89	        ) from exc
90	    with os.fdopen(fd, "w", encoding="utf-8") as stream:
91	        stream.write(f"pid={os.getpid()}\n")
92	        stream.flush()
93	        os.fsync(stream.fileno())
94	
95	
96	def _publish_role_assembly_failure(cfg: Config, exc: BaseException) -> None:
97	    """Notify the peer when a role fails before its run closure is returned.
98	
99	    Producer notification is allowed only after this process successfully
100	    claimed the attempt path.  That prevents a typo/retry using an occupied
101	    path from poisoning another live producer's channel.
102	    """
103	    env_name = "DISAGG_MANIFEST" if cfg.mode == "offline" else "DISAGG_REF_CHANNEL"
104	    path = os.environ.get(env_name)
105	    if not path:
106	        return
107	    if cfg.training.role == "producer":
108	        claim = _read_control(path + _PRODUCER_CLAIM_SUFFIX)
109	        if claim != f"pid={os.getpid()}":
110	            return
111	        failure_path = path + ".failed"
112	    else:
113	        failure_path = path + ".consumer_failed"
114	    _publish_control_failure(failure_path, exc)
115	
116	
117	def _primary_rank() -> bool:
118	    return int(os.environ.get("RANK", "0")) == 0
119	
120	
121	def _env(name: str) -> str:
122	    value = os.environ.get(name)
123	    if not value:
124	        raise ValueError(f"disaggregated training requires environment variable {name}")
125	    return value
126	
127	
128	def _mooncake_store(cfg: Config, *, retain_on_release: bool = False):
129	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.disaggregated import AuthPolicy
130	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.mooncake_store import MooncakeFeatureStore
131	
132	    token = os.environ.get("DISAGG_AUTH_TOKEN") or None
133	    setup_kwargs = {
134	        "local_hostname": os.environ.get("MOONCAKE_LOCAL_HOSTNAME", "127.0.0.1"),
135	        "metadata_server": _env("MOONCAKE_METADATA_SERVER"),
136	        "master_server_addr": _env("MOONCAKE_MASTER_SERVER_ADDR"),
137	        "protocol": os.environ.get("MOONCAKE_PROTOCOL", "tcp"),
138	        "rdma_devices": os.environ.get("MOONCAKE_RDMA_DEVICES", ""),
139	    }
140	    for env_name, key in (
141	        ("MOONCAKE_GLOBAL_SEGMENT_SIZE", "global_segment_size"),
142	        ("DISAGG_CLIENT_SEGMENT_SIZE", "global_segment_size"),
143	        ("MOONCAKE_LOCAL_BUFFER_SIZE", "local_buffer_size"),
144	        ("DISAGG_CLIENT_BUFFER_SIZE", "local_buffer_size"),
145	    ):
146	        if os.environ.get(env_name):
147	            setup_kwargs[key] = int(os.environ[env_name])
148	    return MooncakeFeatureStore(
149	        store_id=os.environ.get("DISAGG_STORE_ID", cfg.run_id),
150	        setup_kwargs=setup_kwargs,
151	        auth=AuthPolicy(token),
152	        credential=token,
153	        retain_on_release=retain_on_release,
154	    )

157	def _offline_store(cfg: Config, *, retain_on_release: bool = False):
158	    backend = os.environ.get("DISAGG_BACKEND", "shared_dir")
159	    if backend == "mooncake":
160	        return _mooncake_store(cfg, retain_on_release=retain_on_release)
161	    if backend != "shared_dir":
162	        raise ValueError(
163	            f"unknown DISAGG_BACKEND={backend!r}; expected shared_dir or mooncake"
164	        )
165	    from specforge.runtime.data_plane.disaggregated import (
166	        AuthPolicy,
167	        SharedDirFeatureStore,
168	    )
169	
170	    token = os.environ.get("DISAGG_AUTH_TOKEN") or None
171	    return SharedDirFeatureStore(
172	        _env("DISAGG_STORE_ROOT"),
173	        store_id=os.environ.get("DISAGG_STORE_ID", cfg.run_id),
174	        auth=AuthPolicy(token),
175	        credential=token,
176	        retain_on_release=retain_on_release,
177	    )

180	def _server_urls(cfg: Config) -> list[str]:
181	    deployment = cfg.deployment.disaggregated
182	    if deployment is not None and deployment.server_urls:
183	        return list(deployment.server_urls)
184	    raw = os.environ.get("DISAGG_SERVER_URLS") or os.environ.get("DISAGG_SERVER_URL")
185	    if not raw:
186	        raise ValueError(
187	            "online producer requires deployment.disaggregated.server_urls, "
188	            "DISAGG_SERVER_URLS, or DISAGG_SERVER_URL"
189	        )
190	    return [item.strip() for item in raw.split(",") if item.strip()]

193	def _consumer_database_path(cfg: Config) -> Optional[str]:
194	    configured = os.environ.get("DISAGG_DB")
195	    if configured:
196	        return configured
197	    deployment = cfg.deployment.disaggregated
198	    if deployment is None:
199	        return None
200	    state_dir = deployment.consumer_state_dir or deployment.control_dir
201	    return os.path.join(state_dir, "consumer.sqlite")
